mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 100k signals deteriorating labor market conditions, increasing recession risk and depressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS declines exceed 100k, with t-test p < 0.05 for positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) signal weakening demand, creating a negative feedback loop that reduces corporate earnings visibility and drives S&P 500 declines over 60 days.
failure_condition: 60d SPX returns are positive in 55%+ of periods when both INDPRO and HOUST decline, with interaction term coefficient not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 1.5% signals strengthening supply-side capacity utilization, improving corporate margins and driving S&P 500 gains over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO increases exceed 1.5%, with t-test p < 0.05 for negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: A 20bp increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) combined with rising CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% signals tightening monetary policy and inflationary pressures, causing S&P 500 declines over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 55%+ of periods when both FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL > 2.5%, with interaction term coefficient not significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in GDP (GDPC1) above 3% combined with rising home sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, driving equity market optimism and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in 55%+ of periods when both GDPC1 > 3% QoQ and RSAFS rises, with interaction term coefficient not significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate (UNRATE) decline below 5% signals a tight labor market, increasing wage pressures and corporate costs, leading to S&P 500 declines over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE < 5%, with t-test p < 0.05 for positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 95 combined with rising oil prices (not in catalog) would signal supply shocks, but since oil isn't available, we use INDPRO alone to predict S&P 500 declines over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in 55%+ of instances where INDPRO < 95, with t-test p < 0.05 for positive returns.
